items.headline: Messi is now the World Cup's all-time top scorer
items.body: Argentina beat Austria 2-0 in Dallas, and Messi's brace pushed him to 18 career World Cup goals — past Miroslav Klose's old record of 16. He even missed an early penalty before flipping the script.
items.say: Did you catch Messi breaking the all-time World Cup goal record? At 38, in the U.S., of all places.

items.headline: Spain look scary — 4-0 over Saudi Arabia
items.body: The reigning Euro champs made a statement on Sunday, rolling Saudi Arabia 4-0 with teenager Lamine Yamal pulling the strings. They're looking like one of the tournament favorites.
items.say: Spain put up a 4-0 — Yamal's only 18 and already running the show. Hard not to like their chances.

items.headline: Falcons sweep the CS2 Cologne Major
items.body: Team Falcons beat FURIA 3-0 in Sunday's grand final to win their first-ever Counter-Strike Major, never trailing across three maps. Star sniper m0NESY finally got his Major MVP after a long final-stage drought.
items.say: Falcons just 3-0'd the Cologne Major — m0NESY went off. First big trophy for that roster.

items.headline: Knicks win it all — first title since 1973
items.body: New York closed out the Spurs in Game 5 behind 45 points from Finals MVP Jalen Brunson, ending a 53-year championship drought. Roughly two million people packed the parade through downtown Manhattan.
items.say: The Knicks finally did it — first ring since 1973. Brunson dropped 45 to close it out.

items.headline: Hurricanes lift the Cup with a Game 6 shutout
items.body: Carolina blanked the Vegas Golden Knights 3-0 to win the Stanley Cup, their first in 20 years. Captain Jordan Staal took home the Conn Smythe as playoff MVP.
items.say: Carolina finished it with a shutout — Cup number two for the Canes, first in 20 years.

items.headline: Toy Story 5 opens huge — $160M weekend
items.body: Pixar's latest landed the biggest opening of the year, with Woody, Buzz and the gang squaring off against a meddling new tablet named Lilypad. Toy meets tech — and the kids (and parents) showed up.
items.say: Did the kids drag you to Toy Story 5? It just had the biggest opening of the year.

items.headline: House of the Dragon Season 3 is back
items.body: The Targaryen civil war finally goes full-scale, with Season 3 premiering Sunday on HBO Max. Dragons, betrayals, and a whole lot of fire — exactly what fans have been waiting for.
items.say: House of the Dragon's back — Season 3 dropped Sunday. Looks like the war's really on now.

items.headline: Tierra Whack drops 'Whack's Museum'
items.body: The Philly artist released a 12-track project on Juneteenth — no features, all produced by Conductor Williams. It was one of the standouts of New Music Friday, full of her signature genre-bending swerves.
items.say: Have you heard the new Tierra Whack? Whole project, zero features — she's just built different.

items.headline: The Bear comes back for its final season
items.body: FX's stress-cooker of a show returns Thursday for its fifth and final season, all eight episodes dropping at once on Hulu. Carmy's walked away, and the crew has to pull off one last service.
items.say: The Bear's last season hits Thursday — feels like the end of an era. You still watching?

items.headline: Glastonbury kicks off — Coldplay headline for a record 5th time
items.body: Worthy Farm opens Wednesday for the biggest festival of the summer, with Coldplay becoming the first act ever to headline Glastonbury five times. Expect a wild, confetti-soaked singalong.
items.say: Glastonbury starts this week — Coldplay's headlining for a record fifth time. Their live shows are nuts.

links.text: Coldplay live: 'A Sky Full Of Stars'
